title: Pleroma echinatum
intro: Pleroma echinatum, also known as the purple coral tree or coral tree, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleroma echinatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleroma echinatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
taxonomy: Pleroma echinatum is classified in the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecylid. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species. Pleroma echinatum specifically belongs to the genus Pleroma, which is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Pleroma is further classified as part of the order Myrtales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The classification of Pleroma echinatum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
